title: Scrambly Moderation Platform — Moderator & Admin Dashboard

requirements.text: Build a backend data model and actor with the following entities: User (id, username, status: active/warned/banned, warningCount, banReason, banAppealText, banAppealStatus: none/pending/reviewed, isBanned), Moderator (id, principal, name, status: active/banned, banReason), Warning (id, targetUserId, issuedByModeratorId, reason, timestamp), ModeratorReport (id, reportedModeratorId, reportedByUserId, reason, timestamp, status: pending/resolved), and a hardcoded admin identity for 'Jourdain Rodriguez'. Persist all data in stable storage.

quotes: moderators get as much warnings they can give the users
the admin can ban the moderators too

acceptanceCriteria: All entities are stored in stable variables and survive canister upgrades.
Admin identity is locked to 'Jourdain Rodriguez' by name/principal.
Warning records track which moderator issued them and when.
ModeratorReport records track status (pending/resolved).

requirements.text: Implement backend functions for moderator actions: issueWarning(userId, reason) — add a warning to a user with no cap on how many warnings can be given; banUser(userId, reason) — ban a user for disrespectful, unkind, or inappropriate behavior; instantBanUser(userId) — immediately ban a user flagged for pornographic content (ban takes effect instantly, no confirmation step); all three functions must be callable only by authenticated moderators (not banned moderators).

quotes: moderators get as much warnings they can give the users
the moderator can choose to ban

acceptanceCriteria: issueWarning stores a Warning record linked to the moderator's ID.
banUser sets user status to banned with the given reason.
instantBanUser sets user status to banned immediately with reason 'Pornographic content'.
Banned moderators cannot call any moderation function.
Functions return an error if called by a non-moderator identity.

requirements.text: Implement backend functions for the ban appeal system: submitAppeal(userId, appealText) — lets a banned user submit an appeal message; reviewAppeal(userId, decision: approve/deny) — admin-only function to approve (unban) or deny the appeal; getMyAppealStatus(userId) — returns current appeal status for a user.

quotes: banned instantly untill appeal is applied

acceptanceCriteria: A banned user can submit exactly one pending appeal at a time.
On appeal approval, user status is reset to active and ban is lifted.
On appeal denial, ban remains and appeal status is set to 'reviewed'.
Only the admin can call reviewAppeal.
Appeal status is readable by the user and the admin.

requirements.text: Implement backend functions for the report-a-moderator system: reportModerator(moderatorId, reason) — lets a user who received at least one warning report the moderator who issued it; getModeratorReports() — admin-only query to list all pending reports; resolveModeratorReport(reportId, banModerator: Bool) — admin-only function to mark the report resolved and optionally ban the reported moderator.

quotes: if the users get 1 warning for bad reason they can report the moderator for a ban to the admin
the admin can ban the moderator

acceptanceCriteria: Only users who have at least one warning on record can submit a moderator report.
Each report is stored with status 'pending' until the admin resolves it.
When banModerator is true on resolution, the moderator's status is set to banned.
Only the admin can call getModeratorReports and resolveModeratorReport.
A moderator who is banned cannot issue warnings or bans.

requirements.text: Build an admin panel page (accessible only to Jourdain Rodriguez) that shows: (1) a list of all moderators with their status and a 'Ban Moderator' action button for active moderators; (2) a list of all pending moderator reports with reporter info, reason, and 'Ban Moderator' / 'Dismiss' action buttons; (3) a list of all pending ban appeals with user info, appeal text, and 'Approve Appeal' / 'Deny Appeal' action buttons.

quotes: the admin can ban the moderators too
report the moderator for a ban to the admin
the admin can ban the moderator

acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel is only visible/accessible when logged in as Jourdain Rodriguez.
All three sections are visible in the admin panel with live data from the backend.
Clicking 'Ban Moderator' calls the resolveModeratorReport or direct ban function and updates the UI.
Approve/Deny buttons on appeals call the reviewAppeal function and refresh the list.

requirements.text: Build a moderator dashboard page (accessible only to active, non-banned moderators) that shows: (1) a searchable list of all users with their current status (active/warned/banned) and warning count; (2) per-user action buttons: 'Issue Warning' (with a reason input), 'Ban User' (with a reason input), and 'Instant Ban for Pornographic Content' (one-click, no extra input); (3) a disabled/greyed-out state for all action buttons if the moderator is banned.

quotes: moderators get as much warnings they can give the users
the moderator can choose to ban

acceptanceCriteria: Moderator dashboard is only visible to authenticated moderators.
User list updates in real time after any moderation action.
Issue Warning opens an inline form for a reason, then submits.
Ban User opens an inline form for a reason, then submits.
Instant Ban button immediately fires instantBanUser without additional input.
If moderator is banned, all action buttons are disabled with a 'Your account has been suspended' notice.

requirements.text: Build a user-facing status page where a user can: (1) see if they are banned and the reason; (2) submit a ban appeal with a text message if no appeal is currently pending; (3) view their current appeal status (none / pending / approved / denied); (4) view their list of received warnings with reasons and timestamps; (5) report a moderator (with a reason text field) if they have received at least one warning — this submits a moderator report to the admin.

quotes: banned instantly untill appeal is applied
if the users get 1 warning for bad reason they can report the moderator for a ban to the admin

acceptanceCriteria: Banned users see a clear 'You are banned' notice with the reason.
Appeal form is shown only when user is banned and has no pending appeal.
Appeal status ('Pending review', 'Approved', 'Denied') is shown when an appeal exists.
Warning history shows each warning's reason and date.
'Report a Moderator' section is visible only if user has at least one warning and allows selecting which moderator to report with a reason.

requirements.text: Apply a bold, dark-themed visual design across the entire app: deep charcoal/near-black backgrounds, high-contrast accent color (e.g., red-orange for destructive actions, teal/green for approvals), clean sans-serif typography, card-based layout for lists and panels, and clear visual hierarchy that communicates authority and safety for a moderation context.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages share a consistent color palette and typography.
Destructive actions (ban, instant ban) use a red or red-orange color.
Positive actions (approve appeal, dismiss report) use a teal or green color.
Layout is clean, card-based, and usable on desktop screens.

constraints: The admin identity is exclusively 'Jourdain Rodriguez' — no other user can access admin functions.
Banned moderators must not be able to perform any moderation actions.
Pornographic content bans must be instant with no extra confirmation step required from the moderator.
Do not expose the caffeineAdminToken URL in any part of the application.
nonGoals: Real-time chat or messaging between moderators and users.
Automated content scanning or AI moderation.
Multiple admin accounts — only one admin (Jourdain Rodriguez) is supported.
OAuth or third-party login — only Internet Identity is supported.

required: Dark shield badge logo with a stylized 'S' monogram, authority-themed moderation emblem (scrambly-mod-logo.dim_256x256.png)
